_doc: Canonical netflag/netport body-orientation spec. The flag BODY must point
OUTWARD along the stub direction (顺着导线朝外); the wire enters from the
circuit side. This file is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH — never hand-edit the
12 numbers in frozenTable; they are DERIVED from rotationCycle + bodyAnchor.
Both consumers derive the same table:
- skills/easyeda-agent/scripts/orient.py  (the linter's check)
- extension/src/actions.ts deriveBodyRotation()  (what connect_pin writes)
tests/run.py asserts derive(cycle, anchors) == frozenTable, so the two
language copies can never drift. calibrate.js re-validates the 3 anchors +
cycle against live getPrimitivesBBox ground truth (run after importing a new
.eext). See docs/schematic-layout-conventions.md §3.5.
2026-06-29 VERTICAL FIX: on the y-DOWN 2026-06 build (EasyEDA 3.2.149,
connector 0.5.21) a flag body renders with up/down FLIPPED vs the prior y-UP
cycle — confirmed by getPrimitivesBBox on REAL, settled, wired flags (a
connect_pin --direction down ground rendered its bars TOWARD the pin; power
--direction up the same). Horizontal (left/right) was unaffected. The old
table slipped through because calibrate.js read body direction in y-UP and so
could not see the vertical flip (fixed there too). Cycle reversed to
up->right->down->left with power/ground anchors swapped so ONLY the up/down
numbers move; every left/right number is byte-identical to before.
